recalls.Description:
Cubimana Island Storm 3 In 1 Building Sets are being recalled due to a serious risk of injury or death from battery ingestion. These sets include 781 colorful building blocks and are packaged in a black box featuring images of a pirate base and ship. The model number "HG1004" is displayed on the front of the box. The battery compartment in the LED light piece is accessible to children, posing a significant safety concern.

If you own these building sets, it is crucial to take them away from children immediately and cease usage. The button cell batteries can cause severe internal injuries if swallowed. Consumers are advised to properly dispose of the batteries and the product itself. To receive a full refund, send a photo of the disposed product to productrecall@cubimanatoys.com. Ensure that you follow local hazardous waste disposal guidelines for the batteries.
recalls.Title: Cubimana Island Storm 3 In 1 Building Sets Recalled Due to Battery Ingestion Hazard
recalls.ConsumerContact: Contact RBS Toys via email at productrecall@cubimanatoys.com.

Products.Name: Cubimana Island Storm 3 In 1 Building Sets with LED Lights

Recalled Cubimana Pirate Ship Building Set box (back)
Recalled Cubimana Pirate Ship Building Set box (top)
Recalled Cubimana Pirate Ship Building Set box (left side)
Recalled Cubimana Pirate Ship Building Set and battery compartment

Retailers.Name: Available online at Amazon.com from October 2025 to January 2026 for approximately $30.

Distributors.Name: Shenzhen Ruibosi Technology Co., Ltd., dba RBS Toys, of China

Hazards.Name: Risk of serious injury or death from battery ingestion

Remedies.Name: Return the product for a full refund after proper disposal

recalls.metaDescription: Recall of Cubimana Island Storm 3 In 1 Building Sets due to battery ingestion risk. Immediate action required for safety.

recalls.Description: Huaker has issued a recall for its Magnetic Balls and Rods sets, which contain 88 pieces and are packaged in a plastic box with a handle. The packaging features the name "Magnetic Sticks" and the model number "20A-13," but the magnetic toys themselves lack any identifying marks. These sets pose a significant choking risk to young children due to the presence of small balls, violating safety standards for toys intended for children under three years old.
recalls.Title: Huaker Magnetic Balls and Rods Sets Recalled Due to Choking Hazard
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Huaker at Huaker-magnetic-balls@outlook.com.

Products.Name: Huaker Magnetic Balls and Rods Sets

Images.Caption: Recalled Huaker Magnetic Balls and Rods Set
Recalled Huaker Magnetic Balls and Rods Set
Recalled Huaker Magnetic Balls and Rods Set

Retailers.Name: Available online at Amazon.com from September 2025 to November 2025, priced around $23.

Distributors.Name: Shenzhen Huakechuang Technology Co. Ltd., dba Huaker, of China

Hazards.Name: Choking hazard due to small balls in the toy set.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should immediately stop using the recalled sets, keep them away from children, and reach out to Huaker for a full refund. They will need to dispose of the sets and send a photo of the discarded product to Huaker-magnetic-balls@outlook.com.

recalls.metaDescription: Huaker recalls Magnetic Balls and Rods sets due to choking hazards. Stop use immediately and contact for a refund.

recalls.Description: Cubimana treehouse building toy sets are being recalled due to a serious risk of injury or death from battery ingestion. These sets include 618 colorful pieces designed to create a three-tier treehouse that lights up. A notable feature is a clear piece with an LED light, which is connected via wire to a brown component containing a button cell battery. The product packaging prominently displays the Cubimana logo and the words "Tree House," along with the identifier "NO. HG1015" and the total piece count.
recalls.Title: Cubimana Treehouse Building Toy Sets Recalled Due to Battery Ingestion Hazard
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should reach out to Vatos Toys via email at shein.us@vatostoys.com for assistance.

Images.Caption: Recalled Cubimana Treehouse Building Set
Recalled Cubimana Treehouse Building Set - brown building piece with a LED light that uses a button cell battery
Recalled Cubimana Treehouse Building Set - front of the packaging
Recalled Cubimana Treehouse Building Set - back of the packaging

Retailers.Name: Available for purchase online at Shein.com from July 2025 through October 2025 for approximately $20.

Distributors.Name: Shenzhen Excellence Qichuang Technology, dba Vatos Toys, of China

Hazards.Name: The toy sets do not meet mandatory safety standards, as the battery compartment in the LED light is accessible to children. Ingesting button cell or coin batteries can lead to severe injuries, internal chemical burns, or even death.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must stop using the recalled toy sets immediately, keep them away from children, and safely remove and dispose of the batteries. To obtain a full refund, contact Vatos Toys. Additionally, consumers should discard the LED light piece and send a photo of it in the trash to shein.us@vatostoys.com. Note: Button cell and coin batteries are dangerous and should be disposed of or recycled according to local hazardous waste guidelines.

recalls.metaDescription: Cubimana treehouse toy sets recalled due to battery ingestion risk. Immediate action required for safety.

recalls.Description: CreateOn is recalling its Crayola-branded pip-Cubes, magnetic building cubes available in Bold Colors and Glitter sets of 24 or 27 cubes. The Bold Colors sets feature red, green, blue, and yellow cubes, while the Glitter sets include purple, pink, blue, and green cubes adorned with glitter. Each cube is marked with the 'Crayola' logo on the left side. The model and UPC numbers can be found on the bottom of the product packaging.
recalls.Title: CreateOn Recalls Crayola Pip-Cubes Due to Magnet Ingestion Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can contact CreateOn at 800-333-0549 from 9:30 a.m. to 4 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, via email at pipcuberecall@CreateOn.com, or visit https://www.createon.com/recall/ for more information.

Recalled CreateOn Crayola-branded Glitter pip-Cubes, 24-Piece, Model 1000243
Recalled CreateOn Crayola-branded Glitter pip-Cubes, 27-Piece, Model 1000250
Recalled CreateOn Crayola-branded Glitter pip-Cubes, 27-Piece, Model 1000250

Retailers.Name: Available at Michael's Stores nationwide and online at Michaels.com for approximately $20, and at Amazon.com for about $35.

Hazards.Name: Risk of ingestion from loose magnets

recalls.metaDescription: CreateOn recalls Crayola pip-Cubes due to serious ingestion hazards. Learn how to get a replacement set.

recalls.Description: Intellio Toys is recalling its Bright Builder fort kits, which include models FKL-BG, FKL-BR, L-5BG, and L-5BR. These kits, available in green and blue or red and blue, consist of 100 pieces, including long and short sticks, connecting tubes, multilink balls, and a set of five flashlights containing button cell batteries. The recall specifically affects kits that come with flashlights, which are marked with 'Bright Builder' on the box and 'Intellio Toys' on the side. The model number can be found on the bottom right corner of the back of the box.
recalls.Title: Intellio Toys Recalls Bright Builder Fort Kits Due to Ingestion Risk from Flashlights
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should contact Intellio Toys at 888-571-0196 from 10 a.m. to 6 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or email recall@intelliotoys.com. More information can be found online at https://intelliotoys.com/pages/recall-information?_pos=1&_sid=c95ece1a9&_ss=r or by visiting www.intelliotoys.com and clicking on 'Recall' at the top of the page.

Products.Name: Bright Builder Fort Kits with Flashlights

Images.Caption: Recalled Bright Builder Fort Kit
Recalled Bright Builder Fort Kit - box

The recalled Intellio Toys' Bright Builder fort kit has 100 pieces and a pack of five flashlights with button cell batteries. A photo of the flashlights appears on the back of the recalled Bright Builder fort kits.
Five-flashlight package sold with the recalled fort kits or as an add-on component

Injuries.Name: Intellio Toys has received one report of the batteries coming out the flashlight while handling it. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available on Amazon.com and Intelliotoys.com from December 2022 through August 2024, priced between $38 and $49 for the kits and $14 to $15 for the flashlights.

Hazards.Name: Ingestion hazard due to easily accessible button cell batteries in flashlights

Remedies.Name: Stop using the flashlights, keep them away from children, and contact Intellio Toys for a refund of $10 to $15. Dispose of button cell batteries properly.

recalls.metaDescription: Intellio Toys recalls Bright Builder Fort Kits due to ingestion risk from flashlights containing button cell batteries. Contact for refunds.
